Clinical Data Acquisition Standards Harmonization (CDASH) field name.  From the NIH: CDASH establishes a standard way to collect data consistently across studies and sponsors so that data collection formats and structures provide clear traceability of submission data into the Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM), delivering more transparency to regulators and others who conduct data review.

Type of multi-record procedure. Can be following: 0 - S_CARRY_FORWARD_NO, a multirecord procedure that is not permanent 1 - S_CARRY_FORWARD_YES, records are carried forward from visit to visit, it's a legacy value and it not used for new studies 2 - S_CARRY_FORWARD_PERMANENT, the set of records of this procedure persist for the duration of the study null - not a multi-record procedure

A comma-separated list of the fields disabled in the study procedure by this specific procedure rule. For example, if your study_procedure_rule is 'alcohol_status = 'Never consumed', then this field specifies to disable the questions with variable_names matching 'date_stopped, amount_former, years_drinking, amount_current', because those questions do not need to be asked if the answer was that alcohol was never consumed. Each listed fields matches a variable_name from the study_procedure_question table.
